channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dog poo, 19th May
item.description:
Dog poo

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Albion Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large areas of graffiti on garden fences and walls, 19th May
item.description:
Reference 933986 dated 22.4 2026. I may be getting my knickers in a twist but the grafitti has NOT been fixed. And as I had feared More and more has appeared. Most of it is large and bright And doesn’t look quite right Down a lane with a country feel A railway track would be ideal.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Kynaston Road, Ramsden, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sidewalk and drop curb tarmac cracked and uneven, causing safety issue for residents and pedestrians, 19th May
item.description:
This sidewalk and drop curb tarmac has significantly deteriorated over a number of years due to numerous excavation works carried out. The surface is now cracked, very uneven, and breaking apart in several areas, creating ongoing safety hazards for residents and pedestrians, particularly elderly people, children, and those with mobility difficulties. During wet weather, the damaged surface becomes slippery, very dangerous and increases the risk of injury, trips and falls. This issue has been left unresolved for several years and the condition continues to worsen. I duly request that these be inspected and fully resurfaced as soon as possible to restore a safe and accessible pathway for the community.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38, Ravensbury Road, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road blocked, 19th May
item.description:
This has been like it for two days blocking three spaces from 105-99 are they allowed to do this?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 91, Harberton Road, Archway, Greater London

item.title: Fly Tipping Truman’s Road, 19th May
item.description:
A mattress, which was previously in the parking lot, has now been dumped on Truman’s Road behind the pay and display stand. Harsher punishment is needed as whoever manages the parking lot constantly reoffends.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salcombe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged pavement Porthkerry avenue Welling, 19th May
item.description:
Damaged pavement Porthkerry avenue Welling Resident reporting damaged pavement running all the way through on porthkerry avenue. she is worried someone will fall and hurt them selves.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Porthkerry Avenue, Welling, Greater London

item.title: Rats infestation, 19th May
item.description:
This needs to be fixed as a priority! Many rats running around between the buildings and the green area.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Creek Road, Deptford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Priory Gardens: Water Supply Cut Off, 19th May
item.description:
Mon 18 May 2026: Since the depot area has become a construction site, and access to the water tap in the depot area increasingly difficult, we now find that the water supply to the tap in the formal gardens appears to have been cut off putting the addition of new plants in jeopardy. Please rectify as a matter of urgency. TIA

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Hill, Goddington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road markings are virtually invisible, 19th May
item.description:
Road markings are badly worn away especially the centre line of the road also could do with lines painted at exit from rear of shops and the access road to the rear of the property’s of Cray Avenue so as to discourage drivers from parking and blocking access and also causing problems for the lorries to access the road to rear of shops

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St. Andrew's Drive, Ramsden, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The green food waste bin at our Bexleyheath site is being used by the public for fly tipping, leaving it contaminated, infested with maggots, and uncollectable, which is now causing complaints and hygiene concerns. We are requesting support from the local authority and the Environmental Health team, especially as a Subway food business also shares this yard and the situation is attracting rodent activity., 19th May
item.description:
We would like to report an ongoing fly tipping and waste management issue at our Bexleyheath site and request urgent assistance from the local authority. For several months, the bins, particularly the green food waste bin, have been repeatedly misused by members of the public as a dumping ground. As a result, the food bin is now heavily contaminated, infested with maggots, and producing a strong odour. Waste crews have refused to empty it due to the condition it is being left in, and our cleaner is unable to handle it safely. This situation is now causing complaints from our tenants and neighbouring businesses, and we are concerned about the escalating environmental and hygiene risks. We would appreciate support in addressing the misuse of the bin area and preventing further public dumping. Given the presence of maggot activity, rodent attraction, and decomposing food waste, we believe this matter also requires the attention of the Environmental Health team. The yard is shared by multiple businesses, including a Subway food premises, which increases the urgency of ensuring the area is kept hygienic and compliant with food safety expectations. We are requesting guidance and intervention to help restore safe waste management conditions, prevent further fly tipping, and ensure the site does not pose a health risk to staff, customers, or the wider community.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broadway, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant flytipping in Durfey Place, 19th May
item.description:
Flytipping in Durfey Place remains a constant issue. I have already raised this within several complaints. It is now happening almost every day, and it continues to have a real impact on residents. Ms Sophia Opoku-Addo, Resident Services Officer for this area, never replied to any emails about the issue and the working group set up by her predecessor. This is simply unacceptable.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Durfey Place, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter all down Hassett td, 19th May
item.description:
The street cleaner literally just walked past litter all way down street. Apart from sweeping or kicking occasional piece into road left rest, to blown into people’s gardens si they can clear up daily.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ballance Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
